title: Midnight-Salty Korean Meringue Bao
description: A low-glycemic-friendly Korean dessert featuring molten meringue nests, infused with savory saltiness.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together buckwheat and almond flours.
In a separate bowl, whip the egg whites until frothy.
Add granulated sugar substitute to the egg whites and continue whipping until stiff peaks form.
Add coconut oil, Korean chili flakes, and salt to the egg whites and mix until combined.
Gradually add the flour mixture to the egg mixture and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Preheat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and cook the dough circles for 1 minute on each side, until golden brown.
Transfer the cooked dough circles to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and top each circle with a spoonful of granulated sugar substitute.
Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F (200°C) for 10-12 minutes, until golden brown and puffed.
Remove from oven and immediately drizzle with infused sesame oil and sprinkle with dark brown sugar substitute and mashed black sesame seeds.
Serve warm, garnished with a pinch of salt and a sprinkle of Korean chili flakes.
